bead.title: worker: bound route resolution and routing preflight with a configurable timeout and release on expiry
bead.description:
PROBLEM
ddx work --watch can claim a bead and hang indefinitely in route resolution / routing preflight: heartbeats keep ticking (phase=running) but harness/model/route stay empty and no LLM call happens (parent ddx-8f2e0ebf; Fizeau 2026-05-18, attempt 20260518T020552-18b086b2 wedged 80+ min). The route-adjacent calls run on a bare context with no per-operation deadline.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1074-1075 — RoutePreflight (field declared at :68, `RoutePreflight func(ctx context.Context, harness, model string) error`) is invoked with the top-level ctx and no timeout.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4913 — CheckAndApplyRouteExclusions calls `resolveRoute(ctx, req)` with a bare ctx.
Neither is bounded, so a hung resolver wedges the single-threaded worker for hours while the lease is held; only top-level ctx cancellation (loop level) can interrupt it.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add a configurable route-resolution timeout (default 60s): a config knob plus an ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime field defaulting to 60s.
2. Wrap RoutePreflight (:1074) and the resolveRoute viability check (:4913) in context.WithTimeout(ctx, routeResolutionTimeout).
3. On timeout: release the lease via the atomic release path (Store.Release from child ddx-449baa1d), set the attempt/report status to execution_failed with reason route_resolution_timeout, and emit an operator-attention event carrying bead-id, attempt-id, last_activity_at, and a short diagnosis. Do NOT auto-retry without operator attention.

NON-SCOPE
Phase-empty heartbeat watchdog (child B), same-bead-twice guard (child D), operator-attention query surface + docs (child E). The atomic release primitive comes from child ddx-449baa1d (dependency). This is a decomposition child of ddx-8f2e0ebf.
bead.acceptance:
1. A configurable route-resolution timeout (default 60s) bounds both routing preflight (execute_bead_loop.go:1074) and the resolveRoute viability check (execute_bead_loop.go:4913); the 60s default is asserted by TestRouteResolutionTimeoutDefaultIs60s.
2. When route resolution / preflight exceeds the timeout, the worker releases the lease via the atomic release path, the attempt/report status is execution_failed with reason route_resolution_timeout, and an operator-attention event is emitted with bead-id, attempt-id, last_activity_at, and a diagnosis. Verified by an integration test TestWorkerReleasesOnRouteResolutionTimeout that injects a hanging resolveRoute stub and asserts release within timeout+10s.
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes
